title: Understanding Displacement
text: Displacement is the change in position of the particle. It is calculated as the final position minus the initial position.

title: Find Initial and Final Positions
text: Use the position function $$x(t)=4-2t$$ to find the position of the particle at $$t=3.0s$$ and $$t=6.0s$$.

title: Calculate Initial Position
text: Substitute $$t=3.0$$ into the position function $$x(t)=4-2t$$ to find the initial position.

title: Calculate Final Position
text: Substitute $$t=6.0$$ into the position function $$x(t)=4-2t$$ to find the final position.

title: Calculate Displacement
text: Use the formula for displacement: final position minus initial position. Use your results from the previous steps.
